title:		Integrated Test  
subtitle:	MultiMarkdown test suite  
author:		Fletcher T. Penney  

This file is a designed as a single test that incorporates most of
the *commonly* used MultiMarkdown features. This allows a single test file to
identify common structures that are not supported yet -- particularly useful
when developing a new output format.


Core Markdown Features
======================

Basic Block Level Elements
--------------------------

### Paragraphs ###

Normal paragraphs consist of plain text.

They are separated by empty lines.

Long lines of text will wrap naturally as needed to ensure that they fit in the available space.  This will vary by format, of course.

By including two spaces at the end of a line  
a linebreak is inserted.

MultiMarkdown adds the ability to use\
an "escaped newline" for this as well.


### Headers ###

ATX headers and Setext headers are used in this document

	## ATX Header 2 ##

	Setext Header 1
	===============

	Setext Header 2
	---------------


### Blockquotes ###

This is a normal paragraph.

> This is a blockquote.
>
> With two paragraphs.
>
>> And a nested blockquote.
>> (Which doesn't work well in Beamer.)


### Unordered Lists ###

Lists can be "tight" or "loose".

Unordered/tight:

* foo
* bar
* bat

Unordered/loose:

* foo

* bar

* bat


### Ordered Lists ###

Lists can be "tight" or "loose".

Ordered/tight:

1. foo
2. bar
3. bat

Ordered/loose:

1. foo

2. bar

3. bat


### Complex Lists ###

Lists can contain other elements

*	A list item.

	With a second paragraph.

*	An item.

	1. With
	2. a
	3. sublist


### Code Blocks ###

Code blocks are indented with 4 spaces or 1 tab.

    This is a code block.

	As is this.


### Horizontal Rules ###

You can include horizontal rules

***

between paragraphs and other elements.


### HTML Blocks ###

HTML blocks can be included, but will work differently when not exporting to HTML, of course.

<div>*foo*</div>

<div>

*bar*

</div>


Basic Span Level Elements
-------------------------

### Links ###

Links can be [inline](http://example.com/inline "Inline Link"), or [reference].

[reference]: http://example.com/reference
	"Reference Link"

You can also use automatic links <http://example.com/auto>
and email <auto@example.com>.


### Emphasis ###

Text can be *emphasized*, **strong**, or ***both***.


### Code ###

You can include `code()` in the middle of other text.


### Images ###

Images are similar to links, but with a leading `!`.

![image]\ 

[image]: test-image.png "Image title"


### Backslash Escapes ###

\*This is not emphasized.\*

\[This is not a link\]\[\].

\* This is not a list item


### Inline HTML ###

HTML can be included within other blocks.

(Markdown does not parse markup inside these HTML spans, whereas MultiMarkdown tries to.)

This is text with <foo>*bar*</foo> HTML.


MultiMarkdown Features
======================

Extended Block Level Elements
-----------------------------

### Metadata ###

Metadata can be included at the top of a document.

	Title:		Integrated Test  
	Subtitle:	MultiMarkdown test suite  
	Author:		Fletcher T. Penney  

It can be referenced with `[%title]` to insert the meta value - [%title].

### Header Cross-References ###

MultiMarkdown automatically generates anchors for headers, to allow for navigation,
such as jumping back to the previous [Metadata][] section.


### Table of Contents ###

MultiMarkdown can generate a TOC from the header structure.

{{TOC}}


### Fenced Code Blocks ###

```
Fenced code blocks are easier in some circumstances.
```


### Definition Lists ###

Term 1
Term 2
:	Definition 1
:	Definition 2


### Tables ###

| foo	| bar	|  
 -----+ | :--:+	|  
| *foo*	| *bar*	|  
| **foo bar**	||  


### Figures ###

An image in a paragraph by itself becomes a figure.

![image2]



Extended Span Level Elements
----------------------------

### Footnotes ###

Foo.[^This is an inline footnote]

Bar.[^foot]

[^foot]: And a reference footnote.


### Citations ###

An inline citation.[#Inline Citation]

A reference citation.[#foo]

[#foo]: bar


### Glossaries ###

[?term]

[?term]: A term to be defined.


### Abbreviations ###

[>MMD] and [>MMD].

[>MMD]: MultiMarkdown


### Image attributes ###

Images can have attributes.

![image2]\ 

[image2]: test-image.png "Image title" width=40px


### Math ###

foo \\({e}^{i\pi }+1=0\\) bar

\\[ {x}_{1,2}=\frac{-b\pm \sqrt{{b}^{2}-4ac}}{2a} \\]

foo ${e}^{i\pi }+1=0$ bar

foo ${e}^{i\pi }+1=0$, bar

$${x}_{1,2}=\frac{-b\pm \sqrt{{b}^{2}-4ac}}{2a}$$


### Smart Quotes ###

"foo" and 'bar' -- with --- dashes...


### CriticMarkup ###

{++Insert++}

{--Delete--}

{~~Old~>New~~}

{>>Comment<<}

{==Highlighted==}
